ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Ka Zugsɔb Wina'am yɛl ye, “Ninsaal la yʋ'ʋn nwɛnɛ tinam nɛ, ka mi' sʋm nɛ bɛ'ɛdi bakid. Li pʋ sʋ'ʋm ye o pun'oe nyɔvʋr tiig la wala di ka vʋe saŋa wʋsaa.”
translated: When the woman's husband died and left her with children and possessions, she had to sell the children to buy food to eat, and they sold them.

ref: Lamech said, “I'll name him Noah because he will give us comfort, as we struggle hard to make a living on this land that the Lord has put under a curse.”
text: ka pʋd o yʋ'ʋr ye Noa, ka yɛl ye, “Bikaŋa na bʋgʋli ti sʋʋnr nɛ namisʋg kanɛ ka ti nyɛt kuobin nɛ ti nu'us tʋʋma wʋsa ni, ka li yinɛ Wina'am da pɔ nɔɔri niŋ teŋ la.”
translated: When he had finished speaking to Noah, he said, “The earth and the sky have been opened, and you must enter the ark with your family and all your household, for the Lord will destroy all the land.”

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Ka dau la gɔsid o kii ka pʋ yɛl si'ela bɔzugɔ, o bɔɔd ye o baŋ ye Zugsɔb la maal o ken la sʋm bɛɛ?
translated: When you all come to the town, what will they say to you all? Are they afraid to hand you over to Zugsɔb, or can they question it?

ref: When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is God's camp.” So he named the place Mahanaim.
text: Ka Jakob n nyɛ ba la o yɛl ye, “Kpɛla anɛ Wina'am nidib la la'asʋg zin'ig!” Ka pʋd zin'ikan la ye Mahanam.
translated: So Jacob said to his brother, "Come, let us set up a heap here today as a witness!" And he named the heap Mahanam.
